---
title: "Theoretical notation / ideas for project"
subtitle: "CSAS 2026"
author: "Naren Prakash"
format: html
---

# Modeling Win/Draw/Loss probabilities

-   Goal: Identify how relegated teams are different from surviving teams (using bookmaker expectations) and looking at **uncertainty** (use statistical entropy)

-   Using bookmaker odds as approximations of latent match probabilities, uncovered using a hierarchical model

    ## Observations

-   Bookmaker probabilities - fixed, point level estimates taken as given information

-   Match outcomes - (Win, draw, loss)

-   Probability vector, $p_i = (p_{i,w},p_{i,d}, p_{i,l})$

    -   Where $p_w$ represents probability of a win (for home side), $p_d$ represents a probability of a draw for both sides, and $p_l$ represents probability of a loss (home side)

    -   $p_i$ represents probability for the ith match (observation)

    -   Probabilities from transformation of Bet365 odds

-   Random variable $y_i$ represents the match outcome for each match i

    -   Thus, we define $y_i$ to be distributed as $Categorical(p_i)$, a generalization of the Bernoulli distribution for the three match end states

-   $t$ represents the season of interest

-   $h(i)$ or $a(i)$ represents the home and away indicator functions for match i
-  $r_t$ represents the relegation status of the home team for season t

## Latent variables
- $\pi_i$ represents the the latent probability vector for match i, with $\pi_i = (\pi_{i,w}, \pi_{i,d}, \pi_{i,l})$ where w,d,l correspond to the win/draw/loss probabilities for the home team using betting odd probabilities
- $\theta_{c, t}$ for club $c$ in season $t$ is the latent team strength
